review: Compare with source-controlled workflow and publish intentionally after review.

workflow.name: RB Calls ElevenLabs Events

parameters.content: Receives ElevenLabs post-call and failure webhooks, plus runs a one-minute no-answer watchdog so accepted outbound calls that never start a human conversation do not remain locked. Routine post-call and sweep state changes update Notion only; Slack is reserved for live human-help requests. Uses ElevenLabs static egress IP allowlist; add HMAC validation before production if n8n raw-body support is available.

blockValues.textContent: ={{ ["Status: provisional", "Source: ElevenLabs event " + $json.event_type + ($json.conversation_id ? " for conversation " + $json.conversation_id : ""), "Imported: " + $now.toISO(), "Review: Full transcript is stored in this page body. The Transcript property is only an excerpt because Notion rich-text properties are capped."].join("\n") }}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { status: "received", call_id: $("Normalize ElevenLabs Event").item.json.call_page_id, conversation_id: $("Normalize ElevenLabs Event").item.json.conversation_id } }}

blockValues.textContent: ={{ ["Status: provisional", "Source: ElevenLabs conversation status sweep" + ($json.conversation_id ? " for conversation " + $json.conversation_id : ""), "Imported: " + $now.toISO(), "Review: Full transcript is stored in this page body. The Transcript property is only an excerpt because Notion rich-text properties are capped."].join("\n") }}

workflow.description: Stores ElevenLabs post-call/sweep outcomes in Notion, treats IVR dead-ends as follow-up, and keeps call status updates from being blocked by note creation.

workflow.triggerInfo:
This workflow has the following trigger(s):


Webhook trigger(s):


				<trigger 1>
					 - Node name: ElevenLabs Events Webhook
					 - Base URL: https://eipventures.app.n8n.cloud/
					 - Production path: /webhook/rb-calls-elevenlabs-events
					 - Test path: /webhook-test/rb-calls-elevenlabs-events
					 - HTTP Method: POST
					 - Response Mode: Webhook is configured to respond using "Respond to Webhook" node.
					 - No credentials required for this webhook.
				</trigger 1>

Schedule trigger(s):


				<trigger 1>
					 - Node name: Every minute - no-answer watchdog
				</trigger 1>

Scheduled workflows can be executed directly through MCP clients and do not require external inputs.
